column1: One row per onboarding field. This is the contract between the app wizard and the backend: raw_answer is what the user taps, canonical_value is what gets stored and read by the generator. Mappings are deterministic lookups in code, never inferred by the AI at generation time, so two users who tap identical answers always route identically. 'ask' fields must be collected in the wizard. 'infer_ok' fields may be defaulted and refined later in app.

column3: Build muscle | Lose fat | Get stronger | Stay healthy

column1: - Screening is the final onboarding step (step 12), placed after motivation but BEFORE the first program is generated. Generator Flow step 1 reads the stored screening outcome, so the screen must be completed at the end of onboarding and the result saved before generation runs. It gates generation via Safety Rule S05.

- Screening is a real screen, not a Terms of Use clause: the answers must change what the app does, which a buried waiver cannot. The waiver checkbox sits on the same screen, on top of it, not instead of it.

- REQUIRE_PROFESSIONAL_CLEARANCE and DO_NOT_GENERATE outcomes stop generation. The app shows a neutral message directing the user to a health professional and unlocks generation only after clearance is confirmed. No diagnosis language.
- The 'anything else' free text on step 8 is an addition, never a replacement for the seven structured questions. It catches extra detail; it does not route on its own.
- Every 'ask' row must be present before step 3 of the Generator Flow runs. Every 'infer_ok' row may be defaulted at generation and refined later in conversation with the AI.
- All canonical mappings live in one code lookup, shared by the wizard and the generator, so onboarding answers and backend behaviour can never drift.
